use_modus_ponens_p_q_and_p_gives_q: Use Modus Ponens: P→Q and P gives Q
use_modus_tollens_p_q_and_q_gives_p: Use Modus Tollens: P→Q and ¬Q gives ¬P
hs_chains_p_q_and_q_r_then_mp_with_p: HS chains P→Q and Q→R, then MP with P
disjunctive_syllogism_p_q_and_p_gives_: Disjunctive Syllogism: P∨Q and ¬P gives Q
conjunction_gives_p_q_then_modus_ponen: Conjunction gives P∧Q, then Modus Ponens gives R
chain_p_q_and_q_r_with_hs_then_again_w: Chain P→Q and Q→R with HS, then again with R→S, then MP
mt_on_p_q_gives_p_then_ds_on_p_r_gives: MT on P→Q gives ¬P, then DS on P∨R gives R
hs_gives_p_r_then_mt_with_r_gives_p: HS gives P→R, then MT with ¬R gives ¬P
truth_tables_every_input_combination_e: Truth Tables — every input combination, every output
for_n_variables_the_table_has_2_rows_a: For n variables, the table has 2ⁿ rows. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF — each gate is just a column. Boolean algebra (Boole 1854) underlies every digital circuit, every search query, every conditional in code.
proof_builder_derive_conclusions_step_: Proof Builder — derive conclusions step by step
modus_ponens_modus_tollens_hypothetica: Modus ponens, modus tollens, hypothetical syllogism, disjunctive syllogism — the eight inference rules cover most introductory propositional logic. Each step justifies itself by NAME, not vibe.
tautology_always_true_contradiction_al: Tautology = always true; contradiction = always false; contingency = sometimes both. Aristotle’s law of non-contradiction (350 BCE) is the oldest published rule. AP CS Principles practice + intro discrete math.
logic_gates_transistors_all_the_way_do: Logic Gates — transistors all the way down
nand_is_functionally_complete_you_can_: NAND is functionally complete — you can build every other gate from NAND alone. A modern CPU contains ~10 billion transistors implementing the same boolean math you’re building here.
p_and_q_each_carry_a_probability_inste: P and Q each carry a probability instead of a hard true/false. For independent events, P(P∧Q) = P(P) × P(Q). Watch how a confidence threshold turns soft probabilities into discrete inferences.
drag_tiles_or_click_to_add: Drag tiles or click to add
drop_symbols_here_or_click_above: Drop symbols here or click above…
enter_an_expression_above_to_generate_: Enter an expression above to generate a truth table
can_we_conclude_the_butler_is_innocent: Can we conclude the butler is innocent?
will_it_go_in_the_display_case: Will it go in the display case?
can_we_conclude_the_creature_has_no_wi: Can we conclude the creature has no wings?
is_the_gold_in_cave_b: Is the gold in Cave B?
can_we_conclude_no_dark_clouds: Can we conclude no dark clouds?
toggle_inputs_to_see_the_output_live: Toggle inputs to see the output live
sliders_for_p_q_truth_probabilities_co: Sliders for P, Q truth probabilities, confidence threshold. Discrete 4-state inference outcome. No score, no reveal.
independence_vs_correlation_when_does_: Independence vs correlation — when does each apply?
how_does_threshold_change_inference_ou: How does threshold change inference outcome?
i_understand_explain_in_own_words: I understand — explain in own words
design_note_discrete_4_state_inference: Design note: discrete 4-state inference marker; no probability score; no reveal — by design.
toggle_educational_panel_what_is_propo: Toggle educational panel: What is Propositional Logic
propositional_logic_studies_how_the_tr: Propositional logic studies how the truth of complex statements depends on simpler ones. A
is_any_statement_that_is_either_true_o: is any statement that is either true or false — like "It is raining" or "2 + 2 = 4".
argument_guarantees_the_conclusion_if_: argument guarantees the conclusion IF the premises are true. An
argument_fallacy_has_a_logical_gap_eve: argument (fallacy) has a logical gap — even if the premises are true, the conclusion doesn't necessarily follow.
common_fallacies_affirming_the_consequ: Common fallacies: Affirming the Consequent (P→Q, Q ∴ P✗), Denying the Antecedent (P→Q, ¬P ∴ ¬Q✗)
truth_tables_boolean_logic_at_a_glance: 🔍 Truth Tables — Boolean logic at a glance
truth_table_visualization_cycling_thro: Truth table visualization cycling through AND, OR, XOR, and NAND logic gates.
